120	
121	def apply_top_k_top_p_tpu(
122	    logits: torch.Tensor,
123	    k: torch.Tensor,
124	    p: torch.Tensor,
125	) -> torch.Tensor:
126	    """
127	    Apply top-k and top-p optimized for TPU.
128	
129	    This algorithm avoids using torch.scatter which is extremely slow on TPU.
130	    This is achieved by finding a "cut-off" element in the original logit, and
131	    after thresholding the logit using this cut-off, the remaining elements
132	    shall constitute the top-p set.
133	
134	    Note: in the case of tie (i.e. multipple cut-off elements present in the
135	    logit), all tie elements are included in the top-p set. In other words,
136	    this function does not break ties. Instead, these tie tokens have equal
137	    chance of being chosen during final sampling, so we can consider the tie
138	    being broken then.
139	    """
140	    probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1)
141	    probs_sort, _ = probs.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
142	
143	    if k is not None:
144	        top_k_count = probs_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)  # shape: (batch, )
145	        top_k_count = top_k_count.unsqueeze(dim=1)
146	        top_k_cutoff = probs_sort.gather(-1, top_k_count)
147	
148	        # Make sure the no top-k rows are no-op.
149	        no_top_k_mask = (k == logits.shape[1]).unsqueeze(dim=1)
150	        top_k_cutoff.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
151	
152	        elements_to_discard = probs < top_k_cutoff
153	        logits.masked_fill_(elements_to_discard, -float("inf"))
154	
155	    if p is not None:
156	        cumprob = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1)
157	        top_p_mask = cumprob <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
158	        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False  # at least one
159	
160	        top_p_count = top_p_mask.sum(dim=-1).unsqueeze(1)
161	        top_p_cutoff = probs_sort.gather(-1, top_p_count)
162	        elements_to_discard = probs < top_p_cutoff
163	        logits.masked_fill_(elements_to_discard, -float("inf"))
164	
165	    return logits
166	
167	
168	def apply_top_k_top_p(
169	    logits: torch.Tensor,
170	    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
171	    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
172	) -> torch.Tensor:
173	    """Apply top-k and top-p masks to the logits.
174	
175	    If a top-p is used, this function will sort the logits tensor,
176	    which can be slow for large batches.
177	
178	    The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
179	    """
180	    if p is None:
181	        if k is None:
182	            return logits
183	
184	        # Avoid sorting vocab for top-k only case.
185	        return apply_top_k_only(logits, k)
186	
187	    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
188	
189	    if k is not None:
190	        # Apply top-k.
191	        top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)  # shape: B
192	        # Get all the top_k values.
193	        top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
194	        top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
195	        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
196	
197	    if p is not None:
198	        # Apply top-p.
199	        probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
200	        probs_sum = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1, out=probs_sort)
201	        top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
202	        # at least one
203	        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
204	        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
205	
206	    # Re-sort the probabilities.
207	    logits = logits_sort.scatter(dim=-1, index=logits_idx, src=logits_sort)
208	    return logits
209	
210	
211	def apply_top_k_only(
212	    logits: torch.Tensor,
213	    k: torch.Tensor,
214	) -> torch.Tensor:
215	    """
216	    Apply top-k mask to the logits.
217	
218	    This implementation doesn't involve sorting the entire vocab.
219	
220	    The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
221	    """
222	    no_top_k_mask = k == logits.shape[1]
223	    # Set non-top-k rows to 1 so that we can gather.
224	    k = k.masked_fill(no_top_k_mask, 1)
225	    max_top_k = k.max()
226	    # topk.values tensor has shape [batch_size, max_top_k].
227	    # Convert top k to 0-based index in range [0, max_top_k).
228	    k_index = k.sub_(1).unsqueeze(1)
229	    top_k_mask = logits.topk(max_top_k, dim=1).values.gather(1, k_index.long())
230	    # Handle non-topk rows.
231	    top_k_mask.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask.unsqueeze(1), -float("inf"))
232	    logits.masked_fill_(logits < top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
233	    return logits
234

236	def random_sample(
237	    probs: torch.Tensor,
238	    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
239	) -> torch.Tensor:
240	    """Randomly sample from the probabilities.
241	
242	    We use this function instead of torch.multinomial because torch.multinomial
243	    causes CPU-GPU synchronization.
244	    """
245	    q = torch.empty_like(probs)
246	    # NOTE(woosuk): To batch-process the requests without their own seeds,
247	    # which is the common case, we first assume that every request does
248	    # not have its own seed. Then, we overwrite the values for the requests
249	    # that have their own seeds.
250	    if len(generators) != probs.shape[0]:
251	        q.exponential_()
252	    if generators:
253	        # TODO(woosuk): This can be slow because we handle each request
254	        # one by one. Optimize this.
255	        for i, generator in generators.items():
256	            q[i].exponential_(generator=generator)
257	    return probs.div_(q).argmax(dim=-1).view(-1)
258	
259	
260	def flashinfer_sample(
261	    logits: torch.Tensor,
262	    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
263	    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
264	    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
265	) -> torch.Tensor:
266	    """Sample from the logits using FlashInfer.
267	
268	    Statistically, this function is equivalent to the `random_sample` function.
269	    However, this function is faster because it avoids sorting the logits tensor
270	    via rejection sampling.
271	
272	    NOTE: The outputs of this function do not necessarily match the outputs of
273	    the `random_sample` function. It only guarantees that the outputs are
274	    statistically equivalent.
275	
276	    NOTE: This function includes CPU-GPU synchronization, while `random_sample`
277	    does not. Call this function at the end of the forward pass to minimize
278	    the synchronization overhead.
279	    """
280	    assert not (k is None and p is None)
281	    if k is None:
282	        # Top-p only.
283	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
284	        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_p_sampling_from_probs(
285	            probs, p, deterministic=True)
286	    elif p is None:
287	        # Top-k only.
288	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
289	        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_k_sampling_from_probs(
290	            probs, k, deterministic=True)
291	    else:
292	        # Both top-k and top-p.
293	        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_k_top_p_sampling_from_logits(
294	            logits, k, p, deterministic=True)
295	
296	    return next_token_ids.view(-1)

32	def test_topk_impl_equivalence():
33	
34	    torch.set_default_device(DEVICE)
35	    generator = Generator(device=DEVICE).manual_seed(33)
36	
37	    logits = torch.rand((BATCH_SIZE, VOCAB_SIZE), generator=generator)
38	
39	    # Random top-k values between 1 and 9.
40	    k = torch.randint(1, 10, (BATCH_SIZE, ), generator=generator)
41	
42	    # Set k=vocab_size for ~50% of requests in the batch (top-k disabled).
43	    k.masked_fill_(
44	        torch.randint(0, 2, (BATCH_SIZE, ), generator=generator, dtype=bool),
45	        VOCAB_SIZE)
46	
47	    # Top-k only implementation
48	    result1 = apply_top_k_top_p(logits=logits.clone(), k=k, p=None)
49	
50	    # Top-p + top-k
51	    no_op_top_p = torch.tensor([1.0])
52	    result2 = apply_top_k_top_p(logits=logits.clone(), k=k, p=no_op_top_p)
53	
54	    assert torch.allclose(result1, result2)

336	    @property
337	    def _should_modify_greedy_probs_inplace(self) -> bool:
338	        """Whether or not the sampler should modify the probability distribution
339	        of greedily-sampled tokens such that multinomial sampling would sample
340	        the greedily-sampled token.
341	
342	        In other words, if True then we set the probability of the greedily-
343	        sampled token to 1.
344	
345	        This is used by speculative decoding, which requires that the sampling
346	        method be encoded into the probability distribution.
347	        """
348	        return self.should_modify_greedy_probs_inplace

169	class Sampler(nn.Module):
170	    """Samples the next tokens from the model's outputs.
171	
172	    This layer does the following:
173	    1. Discard the hidden states that are not used for sampling (i.e., all
174	        tokens except the final one in each prompt).
175	    2. Compute the logits for the next tokens.
176	    3. Apply presence, frequency and repetition penalties.
177	    4. Apply temperature scaling.
178	    5. Apply top-p and top-k truncation.
179	    6. Sample the next tokens.
180	    Here, each sequence group within the batch can have different sampling
181	    parameters (e.g., sampling method, temperature, top-p, top-k, etc.).
182	
183	    The structure of the logits tensor is coupled with the seq_groups in
184	    sampling_metadata. Typically, each sequence in each seq_group has one row in
185	    logits for the next token to be sampled; however, for a seq_group with a
186	    prompt request with the prompt_logprobs sampling parameter, there are rows
187	    in logits for each token in the input prompt.
188	    """
189	
190	    def __init__(self):
191	        super().__init__()
192	
193	        # Whether or not the SamplerOutput should have on-device tensors
194	        # containing the sampled token ids and probabilities. This is used by
195	        # speculative decoding and when prompt embeddings are specified.
196	        self.include_gpu_probs_tensor = False
197	        self.should_modify_greedy_probs_inplace = False
198	
199	    def _init_sampling_tensors(
200	        self,
201	        logits: torch.Tensor,
202	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
203	    ):
204	        """The goal here is to reuse sampling tensors between similar decode
205	        runs. This is possible because sampling logic does not change between
206	        decodes of the same sequences.
207	        """
208	        _, vocab_size = logits.shape
209	
210	        # First free any existing stored sampling tensors.
211	        # This is necessary because some sampling tensors may
212	        # have pinned memory.
213	        self._sampling_tensors = None
214	
215	        # Initialize new sampling tensors
216	        (sampling_tensors, do_penalties, do_top_p_top_k,
217	         do_min_p) = SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata(
218	             sampling_metadata, vocab_size, logits.device, logits.dtype)
219	
220	        self._sampling_tensors = sampling_tensors
221	        self._do_penalties = do_penalties
222	        self._do_top_p_top_k = do_top_p_top_k
223	        self._do_min_p = do_min_p
224	
225	    def forward(
226	        self,
227	        logits: torch.Tensor,
228	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
229	    ) -> Optional[SamplerOutput]:
230	        """
231	        Single-step scheduling:
232	            * Perform GPU-side sampling computation & compute
233	            GPU-side logprobs tensor
234	            * Pythonize sampling result & logprobs tensor
235	
236	        Multi-step scheduling:
237	            * Perform GPU-side sampling computation & compute
238	            GPU-side logprobs tensor
239	            * Defer Pythonization of sampling result & logprobs
240	            tensor
241	            * Encapsulate arguments required for deferred Pythonization
242	            in the
243	            [`SamplerOutput`][vllm.model_executor.layers.sampler.SamplerOutput]
244	            structure
245	
246	        Args:
247	            logits: (num_tokens, vocab_size).
248	            sampling_metadata: Metadata for sampling.
249	        """
250	        assert logits is not None
251	        _, vocab_size = logits.shape
252	
253	        # Prepare sampling tensors with pinned memory to avoid blocking.
254	        if not sampling_metadata.reuse_sampling_tensors:
255	            self._init_sampling_tensors(logits, sampling_metadata)
256	        elif self._do_penalties:
257	            # In this case, the sampling tensors logic depends on
258	            # "output_tokens" of a sequence. As a result, we cannot
259	            # reuse sampling tensors, since "output_tokens" changes
260	            # between decode runs.
261	            self._init_sampling_tensors(logits, sampling_metadata)
262	
263	        assert self._sampling_tensors is not None
264	        sampling_tensors = self._sampling_tensors
265	        do_penalties = self._do_penalties
266	        do_top_p_top_k = self._do_top_p_top_k
267	        do_min_p = self._do_min_p
268	
269	        logits = _apply_min_tokens_penalty(logits, sampling_metadata)
270	
271	        # Apply presence and frequency penalties.
272	        if do_penalties:
273	            logits = apply_penalties(logits, sampling_tensors.prompt_tokens,
274	                                     sampling_tensors.output_tokens,
275	                                     sampling_tensors.presence_penalties,
276	                                     sampling_tensors.frequency_penalties,
277	                                     sampling_tensors.repetition_penalties)
278	
279	        # Use float32 to apply temperature scaling.
280	        # Use in-place division to avoid creating a new tensor.
281	        logits = logits.to(torch.float)
282	        logits.div_(sampling_tensors.temperatures.unsqueeze(dim=1))
283	
284	        if do_top_p_top_k and flashinfer_top_k_top_p_sampling is None:
285	            logits = _apply_top_k_top_p(logits, sampling_tensors.top_ps,
286	                                        sampling_tensors.top_ks)
287	
288	        if do_min_p:
289	            logits = _apply_min_p(logits, sampling_tensors.min_ps)
290	
291	        # We use float32 for probabilities and log probabilities.
292	        # Compute the probabilities.
293	        probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1, dtype=torch.float)
294	        # Compute the log probabilities.
295	        logprobs = torch.log_softmax(logits, dim=-1, dtype=torch.float)
296	
297	        # Sample the next tokens.
298	        maybe_deferred_sample_results, maybe_sampled_tokens_tensor = _sample(
299	            probs,
300	            logprobs,
301	            sampling_metadata,
302	            sampling_tensors,
303	            include_gpu_probs_tensor=self.include_gpu_probs_tensor,
304	            modify_greedy_probs=self._should_modify_greedy_probs_inplace,

351	def _apply_min_tokens_penalty(
352	    logits: torch.Tensor,
353	    sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
354	) -> torch.Tensor:
355	    """Apply min_tokens penalty which sets stop tokens to -inf if min_tokens
356	        have not been generated yet
357	    """
358	    # list of indices in logits that will be set to -inf
359	    logits_to_penalize: list[tuple[int, int]] = []
360	    logits_applied = 0
361	    for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
362	        seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
363	        sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
364	
365	        sample_indices = seq_group.sample_indices
366	        logits_applied += len(sample_indices) + len(
367	            seq_group.prompt_logprob_indices)
368	        if not seq_group.do_sample:
369	            continue
370	
371	        start_idx = sample_indices[0]
372	        min_tokens = sampling_params.min_tokens
373	        token_ids_to_penalize = sampling_params.all_stop_token_ids
374	        if min_tokens > 0 and token_ids_to_penalize:
375	            seqs_to_penalize: list[int] = []
376	            for j, seq_id in enumerate(seq_ids):
377	                seq_data = seq_group.seq_data[seq_id]
378	                if len(seq_data.output_token_ids_array) < min_tokens:
379	                    seqs_to_penalize.append(j)
380	
381	            if seqs_to_penalize:
382	                # convert to the index into logits
383	                seqs_to_penalize = [start_idx + j for j in seqs_to_penalize]
384	                # itertools.product pairs each seq index with every token id
385	                logits_to_penalize.extend(
386	                    itertools.product(seqs_to_penalize, token_ids_to_penalize))
387	
388	    if logits_to_penalize:
389	        # use zip and * to group indices along each dimension
390	        # eg. [ (1,2), (1,3), (5,6) ] -> ( (1,1,5), (2,3,6) )
391	        logits[tuple(zip(*logits_to_penalize))] = -float("inf")
392	
393	    # verifies that no rows in logits were missed unexpectedly
394	    assert logits_applied == logits.shape[0]
395	    return logits

398	def _apply_top_k_top_p(
399	    logits: torch.Tensor,
400	    p: torch.Tensor,
401	    k: torch.Tensor,
402	) -> torch.Tensor:
403	    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
404	
405	    # Apply top-k.
406	    top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
407	    # Get all the top_k values.
408	    top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
409	    top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
410	    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
411	
412	    # Apply top-p.
413	    probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
414	    probs_sum = probs_sort.cumsum(dim=-1)
415	    top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
416	    # at least one
417	    top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
418	    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
419	
420	    # Re-sort the probabilities.
421	    logits = torch.empty_like(logits_sort).scatter_(dim=-1,
422	                                                    index=logits_idx,
423	                                                    src=logits_sort)
424	    return logits
425	
426	
427	def _apply_min_p(
428	    logits: torch.Tensor,
429	    min_p: torch.Tensor,
430	) -> torch.Tensor:
431	    """
432	    Adapted from
433	    https://github.com/oobabooga/text-generation-webui/blob/3146124ec01f02c8fb1650a6517cf1b60b537aaf/modules/sampler_hijack.py#L16C17-L16C17
434	    """
435	    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
436	    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
437	    scaled_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
438	    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_min_p
439	    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
440	
441	    return logits
442

18	    # repetition_penalties=0.0,
19	)
20	
21	
22	@dataclass
23	class TPUSupportedSamplingMetadata:
24	    # This class exposes a more xla-friendly interface than SamplingMetadata
25	    # on TPU, in particular all arguments should be traceable and no optionals
26	    # are allowed, to avoid graph recompilation on Nones.
27	    temperature: torch.Tensor = None
28	
29	    min_p: torch.Tensor = None
30	    top_k: torch.Tensor = None
31	    top_p: torch.Tensor = None
32	
33	    all_greedy: bool = True
34	
35	    # Whether logprobs are to be gathered in this batch of request. To balance
36	    # out compile time and runtime, a fixed `max_number_logprobs` value is used
37	    # when gathering logprobs, regardless of the values specified in the batch.
38	    logprobs: bool = False
39	
40	    # TODO No penalties for now
41	    no_penalties: bool = True
42	    prompt_token_ids = None
43	    frequency_penalties = None
44	    presence_penalties = None
45	    repetition_penalties = None
46	    # should use tensor
47	    output_token_ids: list[list[int]] = field(default_factory=lambda: list())
48	
49	    min_tokens = None  # impl is not vectorized
50	
51	    logit_bias: list[Optional[dict[int, float]]] = field(
52	        default_factory=lambda: list())
53	
54	    allowed_token_ids_mask = None
55	    bad_words_token_ids = None
56	
57	    # Generator not supported by xla
58	    _generators: dict[int,
59	                      torch.Generator] = field(default_factory=lambda: dict())
60	
61	    @property
62	    def generators(self) -> dict[int, torch.Generator]:
63	        # Generator not supported by torch/xla. This field must be immutable.
64	        return self._generators
65	
66	    @classmethod
67	    def from_input_batch(
68	        cls,
69	        input_batch: InputBatch,
70	        padded_num_reqs: int,
71	        xla_device: torch.device,
72	        generate_params_if_all_greedy: bool = False
73	    ) -> "TPUSupportedSamplingMetadata":
74	        """
75	        Copy sampling tensors slices from `input_batch` to on device tensors.
76	
77	        `InputBatch._make_sampling_metadata` causes recompilation on XLA as it 
78	        slices dynamic shapes on device tensors. This impl moves the dynamic 
79	        ops to CPU and produces tensors of fixed `padded_num_reqs` size.
80	
81	        Args:
82	            input_batch: The input batch containing sampling parameters.
83	            padded_num_reqs: The padded number of requests.
84	            xla_device: The XLA device.
85	            generate_params_if_all_greedy: If True, generate sampling parameters
86	                even if all requests are greedy. this is useful for cases where
87	                we want to pre-compile a graph with sampling parameters, even if
88	                they are not strictly needed for greedy decoding.
89	        """
90	        needs_logprobs = input_batch.max_num_logprobs>0 if \
91	            input_batch.max_num_logprobs else False
92	        # Early return to avoid unnecessary cpu to tpu copy
93	        if (input_batch.all_greedy is True
94	                and generate_params_if_all_greedy is False):
95	            return cls(all_greedy=True, logprobs=needs_logprobs)
96	
97	        num_reqs = input_batch.num_reqs
98	
99	        def fill_slice(cpu_tensor: torch.Tensor, fill_val) -> torch.Tensor:
100	            # Pad value is the default one.
101	            cpu_tensor[num_reqs:padded_num_reqs] = fill_val
102	
103	        fill_slice(input_batch.temperature_cpu_tensor,
104	                   DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS["temperature"])
105	        fill_slice(input_batch.min_p_cpu_tensor,
106	                   DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS["min_p"])
107	        fill_slice(input_batch.top_k_cpu_tensor,
108	                   DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS["top_k"])
109	        fill_slice(input_batch.top_p_cpu_tensor,
110	                   DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS["top_p"])
111	
112	        # Slice persistent device tensors to a fixed pre-compiled padded shape.
113	        return cls(
114	            temperature=input_batch.temperature_cpu_tensor[:padded_num_reqs].
115	            to(xla_device),
116	            all_greedy=input_batch.all_greedy,
117	            # TODO enable more and avoid returning None values
118	            top_p=input_batch.top_p_cpu_tensor[:padded_num_reqs].to(
119	                xla_device),
120	            top_k=input_batch.top_k_cpu_tensor[:padded_num_reqs].to(
121	                xla_device),
122	            min_p=input_batch.min_p_cpu_tensor[:padded_num_reqs].to(
123	                xla_device),
124	            logprobs=needs_logprobs)
125

Perfect! Let me now provide a comprehensive report with all findings.

## Comprehensive Analysis: Sampling Parameters in vLLM

Based on my thorough exploration of the vLLM codebase, I've identified exactly how sampling parameters are defined, validated, and documented. Here's a detailed breakdown to guide you in adding the new `top_a` parameter:

### 1. **Primary Definition Location: SamplingParams Class**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775259073450-8zly1x/vllm/sampling_params.py`

#### Field Definitions (Lines 205-215)
```python
n: int = 1
best_of: Optional[int] = None
_real_n: Optional[int] = None
presence_penalty: float = 0.0
frequency_penalty: float = 0.0
repetition_penalty: float = 1.0
temperature: float = 1.0
top_p: float = 1.0          # Line 212
top_k: int = 0              # Line 213
min_p: float = 0.0          # Line 214
seed: Optional[int] = None
```

**Pattern identified:**
- All sampling parameters are class fields with msgspec.Struct decorator
- Float parameters use `float` type with default values
- Default values: `top_p=1.0`, `top_k=0`, `min_p=0.0`

#### Class Docstring (Lines 123-203)
The class uses comprehensive docstrings for each parameter:
- **top_p** (Line 150-151): "Float that controls the cumulative probability of the top tokens to consider. Must be in (0, 1]. Set to 1 to consider all tokens."
- **top_k** (Line 152-153): "Integer that controls the number of top tokens to consider. Set to 0 (or -1) to consider all tokens."
- **min_p** (Line 154-156): "Float that represents the minimum probability for a token to be considered, relative to the probability of the most likely token. Must be in [0, 1]. Set to 0 to disable this."

### 2. **Validation in _verify_args() Method**

**File:** `vllm/sampling_params.py`  
**Lines:** 387-462 (full method)

#### Specific Validations (from grep results):
- **Line 417-418**: `top_p` validation
  ```python
  if not 0.0 < self.top_p <= 1.0:
      raise ValueError(f"top_p must be in (0, 1], got {self.top_p}.")
  ```
  
- **Lines 420-425**: `top_k` validation
  ```python
  if self.top_k < -1:
      raise ValueError(f"top_k must be 0 (disable), or at least 1, "
                       f"got {self.top_k}.")
  if not isinstance(self.top_k, int):
      raise TypeError(
          f"top_k must be an integer, got {type(self.top_k).__name__}")
  ```

- **Lines 426-428**: `min_p` validation
  ```python
  if not 0.0 <= self.min_p <= 1.0:
      raise ValueError("min_p must be in [0, 1], got "
                       f"{self.min_p}.")
  ```

**Validation pattern for float parameters with range [0.0, 1.0]:**
```python
if not 0.0 <= self.min_p <= 1.0:
    raise ValueError("min_p must be in [0, 1], got " f"{self.min_p}.")
```

### 3. **from_optional() Static Method**

**File:** `vllm/sampling_params.py`  
**Lines:** 251-325

This factory method provides an alternative constructor with defaults:
```python
@staticmethod
def from_optional(
    # ... other params ...
    top_p: Optional[float] = 1.0,    # Line 259
    top_k: int = 0,                   # Line 260
    min_p: float = 0.0,               # Line 261
    # ... rest of params ...
) -> "SamplingParams":
```

The method then passes these to the main constructor (lines 292-325).

### 4. **Handling in __post_init__() Method**

**File:** `vllm/sampling_params.py`  
**Lines:** 327-386

Special handling for temperature override (Lines 377-382):
```python
if self.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
    # Zero temperature means greedy sampling.
    self.top_p = 1.0
    self.top_k = 0
    self.min_p = 0.0
    self._verify_greedy_sampling()
```

This resets sampling parameters to defaults when greedy sampling is detected (temperature=0).

### 5. **__repr__() Method Integration**

**File:** `vllm/sampling_params.py`  
**Lines:** 563-588

The __repr__ includes all sampling parameters:

## vLLM Sampler Analysis: top_k, top_p Filtering Flow

I've thoroughly explored the vLLM sampler architecture and identified exactly where and how top_k and top_p filtering are applied. Here's the complete flow:

### 1. SAMPLING PARAMETERS DEFINITION

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775259073450-8zly1x/vllm/sampling_params.py`

**Location:** Lines 118-213

Key fields in `SamplingParams` class:
- `top_p: float = 1.0` (line 212) - cumulative probability threshold, range (0, 1]
- `top_k: int = 0` (line 213) - number of top tokens to consider, 0 means all tokens
- `min_p: float = 0.0` (line 214) - minimum probability relative to max

These are the user-facing sampling parameter definitions that flow into the system.

---

### 2. DATA STRUCTURE FLOW: SamplingParams → SamplingTensors

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775259073450-8zly1x/vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py`

#### A. SequenceGroupToSample (lines 19-61)
Intermediate structure that carries `sampling_params: SamplingParams` for each sequence group during batching.

#### B. SamplingTensors Class (lines 372-597)
Batched tensor representation of sampling parameters:

```
@dataclass
class SamplingTensors:
    temperatures: torch.Tensor      # Line 376
    top_ps: torch.Tensor            # Line 377 - batch of top_p values
    top_ks: torch.Tensor            # Line 378 - batch of top_k values
    min_ps: torch.Tensor            # Line 379 - batch of min_p values
    presence_penalties: torch.Tensor
    frequency_penalties: torch.Tensor
    repetition_penalties: torch.Tensor
    prompt_tokens: torch.Tensor
    output_tokens: torch.Tensor
```

#### C. Conversion Process: SamplingParams → SamplingTensors
**`SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata()` classmethod (lines 386-495)**

Flow:
1. Lines 394-405: Initialize empty lists for batching parameters
2. Lines 408-435: Iterate through seq_groups, extract values:
   - Line 415: `top_p = sampling_params.top_p`
   - Line 419: `top_k = min(sampling_params.top_k, vocab_size)` (clamp to vocab size)

```python
def _apply_top_k_top_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,      # Shape: [batch_size, vocab_size]
    p: torch.Tensor,           # Shape: [batch_size] - top_p values
    k: torch.Tensor,           # Shape: [batch_size] - top_k values
) -> torch.Tensor:
    # Step 1: Sort logits in ascending order
    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
    
    # Step 2: Apply top-k mask
    top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)  # vocab_idx threshold
    top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
    top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask  # mask tokens below top-k
    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
    
    # Step 3: Apply top-p mask (cumulative probability)
    probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
    probs_sum = probs_sort.cumsum(dim=-1)
    top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)  # cumulative <= 1-p
    top_p_mask[:, -1] = False  # ensure at least one token
    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
    
    # Step 4: Re-sort back to original vocabulary order
    logits = torch.empty_like(logits_sort).scatter_(
        dim=-1, index=logits_idx, src=logits_sort)
    return logits
```

**The min_p Filtering: `_apply_min_p()` (lines 427-441)**

```python
def _apply_min_p(logits: torch.Tensor, min_p: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)  # max probability per sequence
    scaled_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs  # min_p * max_prob
    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_min_p  # mask tokens below threshold
    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
    return logits
```

```python
def apply_top_k_top_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],  # Shape: [batch_size]
    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],  # Shape: [batch_size]
) -> torch.Tensor:
    if p is None:
        if k is None:
            return logits  # No filtering
        return apply_top_k_only(logits, k)
    
    # Sort in ascending order
    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
    
    # Apply top-k if present
    if k is not None:
        top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
        top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
        top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
    
    # Apply top-p (cumulative probability)
    if p is not None:
        probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
        probs_sum = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1, out=probs_sort)
        top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False  # ensure at least one token
        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
    
    # Re-sort back to original order
    logits = logits_sort.scatter(dim=-1, index=logits_idx, src=logits_sort)
    return logits
```

```python
def _apply_top_a(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    top_a: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """Apply top-a (nucleus-like) filtering.
    
    Masks tokens where p(token) < top_a * max_probability per sequence.
    
    Args:
        logits: [batch_size, vocab_size]
        top_a: [batch_size] - threshold multiplier (0 to 1)
    """
    # Step 1: Get probabilities
    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
    
    # Step 2: Find max probability per sequence
    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
    
    # Step 3: Compute threshold: top_a * max_prob
    threshold = top_a.unsqueeze(dim=1) * top_probs
    
    # Step 4: Mask tokens below threshold
    tokens_to_remove = probs < threshold
    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
    
    # Step 5: Ensure at least one token (avoid all -inf)
    # Find max logit per sequence after masking
    max_logits = torch.full_like(logits[:, :1], -float("inf"))
    max_logits.scatter_(1, logits.argmax(dim=1, keepdim=True), 1)
    
    return logits
```

### 5. KEY INSIGHTS FOR YOUR top_a IMPLEMENTATION

1. **Execution Order Matters:**
   - Temperature scaling → (top_k + top_p) → min_p → (your top_a)
   - top_a should execute AFTER temperature but placement relative to min_p is flexible

2. **Probability Computation:**
   - Always compute probs from logits each time: `probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)`
   - Don't assume probs are pre-computed from previous filters

3. **Masking Pattern:**
   - Your top_a matches min_p's pattern exactly:
     - Line 436 (min_p): `top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)`
     - Line 437: `scaled_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs`
   - This is the exact pattern you need for top_a

4. **Batching:**
   - Use `.unsqueeze(dim=1)` to broadcast batch values across vocab dimension
   - Shapes: logits [batch, vocab], top_a [batch] → top_a.unsqueeze(dim=1) [batch, 1]

5. **At-Least-One Token:**
   - Both top_p and min_p allow at least one token to remain
   - Consider adding this safeguard in top_a (though implementation differs for min_p/top_p)

```python
        logits: [batch_size, vocab_size]
        a: [batch_size] - threshold multiplier (0 to 1)
    
    Returns:
        logits with tokens below threshold masked to -inf
    """
    probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1)
    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
    threshold = a.unsqueeze(dim=1) * top_probs
    tokens_to_remove = probs < threshold
    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
    return logits
```

Then in the `TopKTopPSampler` class or in `apply_top_k_top_p()`, add a call to this function in the filtering chain.

---

### 5. KEY INSIGHTS FOR YOUR top_a IMPLEMENTATION

1. **Execution Order Matters:**
   - Temperature scaling → (top_k + top_p) → min_p → (your top_a)
   - top_a should execute AFTER temperature but placement relative to min_p is flexible

2. **Probability Computation:**
   - Always compute probs from logits each time: `probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)`
   - Don't assume probs are pre-computed from previous filters

3. **Masking Pattern:**
   - Your top_a matches min_p's pattern exactly:
     - Line 436 (min_p): `top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)`
     - Line 437: `scaled_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs`
   - This is the exact pattern you need for top_a

4. **Batching:**
   - Use `.unsqueeze(dim=1)` to broadcast batch values across vocab dimension
   - Shapes: logits [batch, vocab], top_a [batch] → top_a.unsqueeze(dim=1) [batch, 1]

5. **At-Least-One Token:**
   - Both top_p and min_p allow at least one token to remain
   - Consider adding this safeguard in top_a (though implementation differs for min_p/top_p)

21. `vllm/spec_decode/mlp_speculator_worker.py`

**Tests:**
22. `tests/test_sampling_params.py` - Add validation tests
23. `tests/samplers/test_sampler.py` - Add sampler integration tests
24. `tests/v1/sample/test_sampling_params_e2e.py` - Add V1 end-to-end tests

---

### 9. **Key Data Flow Summary**

```
SamplingParams (user input)
        ↓
ChatCompletionRequest.to_sampling_params()
        ↓
SequenceGroupMetadata (holds SamplingParams)
        ↓
V0 Path:
  SamplingMetadata.prepare()
    ↓
  SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata()
    (extracts top_p/top_k, creates tensors)
    ↓
  Sampler.forward()
    ↓
  _apply_top_k_top_p() or FlashInfer sampler

V1 Path:
  InputBatch._add_request()
    (stores in numpy arrays)
    ↓
  InputBatch._make_sampling_metadata()
    (creates V1 SamplingMetadata with tensors)
    ↓
  V1 Sampler.sample()
    ↓
  TopKTopPSampler kernel
```

This comprehensive mapping ensures that any new sampling parameter follows the same path through both V0 and V1 pipelines!

- **Conditional FlashInfer usage** (lines 284-286):
  - Can use FlashInfer's `top_k_top_p_sampling` if available and enabled
  - Otherwise uses PyTorch implementation

---

### 6. **V1 Pipeline: Modern Architecture**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775259073450-8zly1x/vllm/v1/sample/metadata.py`

**SamplingMetadata (V1)** (lines 12-43):
- Flatter structure compared to V0
- Direct tensor fields:
  - `top_p: Optional[torch.Tensor]` (line 19)
  - `top_k: Optional[torch.Tensor]` (line 20)
  - `temperature: Optional[torch.Tensor]` (line 15)
  - Penalties as tensors
  - Generators dict, logprobs config, bad words, allowed tokens

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775259073450-8zly1x/vllm/v1/worker/gpu_input_batch.py`

**InputBatch** (lines 61-750):
- Manages sampling tensors across batches
- Tensor fields:
  - `top_p` and `top_p_cpu_tensor` (lines 133, 136)
  - `top_k` and `top_k_cpu_tensor` (lines 143, 146)
  - Numpy views: `top_p_cpu`, `top_k_cpu` (lines 140, 150)
  - Tracking sets: `top_p_reqs`, `top_k_reqs` (lines 141, 151)

- When adding requests (line 312-320):
  - `top_p_cpu[req_index] = sampling_params.top_p`
  - `top_k` clamped to vocab_size, stored in `top_k_cpu[req_index]`
  - Adds to tracking sets if non-default

- Method: `_make_sampling_metadata()` (lines 640-648):
  - Creates `SamplingMetadata` with GPU tensors
  - Passes `top_k` and `top_p` (or None if not needed)

- Optimization flags:
  - `no_top_p` property (lines 734-735) - True if all requests have top_p=1.0
  - `no_top_k` property (lines 738-739) - True if all requests have top_k disabled

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775259073450-8zly1x/vllm/v1/sample/tpu/metadata.py`

**TPUSupportedSamplingMetadata** (lines 22-125):
- TPU-specific XLA-friendly version
- Fields: `top_k`, `top_p` (lines 30-31)
- Built from InputBatch in `from_input_batch()` (lines 66-124)

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775259073450-8zly1x/vllm/v1/sample/sampler.py`

**Sampler (V1)** (lines 18-230):
- `forward()` receives V1 `SamplingMetadata` (line 28)
- `sample()` method (lines 90-137):
  - Calls `self.topk_topp_sampler()` with `top_k` and `top_p` tensors (lines 121-126)

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775259073450-8zly1x/vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py`

- Implements actual top-k/top-p filtering kernels
- Called from sampler with `top_k` and `top_p` tensors

---

### 7. **Test Coverage**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775259073450-8zly1x/tests/test_sampling_params.py`
- Tests SamplingParams construction and validation
- Tests conversion from OpenAI API requests

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775259073450-8zly1x/tests/samplers/test_sampler.py`
- Tests sampler with various configs (greedy, random, seeded)
- Creates SamplingMetadata and calls sampler.forward()
- Lines 78-84 show the integration testing pattern

**Additional test files:**
- `tests/samplers/test_rejection_sampler.py`
- `tests/samplers/test_typical_acceptance_sampler.py`
- `tests/v1/sample/test_sampler.py`
- `tests/v1/sample/test_sampling_params_e2e.py`
- `tests/v1/sample/test_topk_topp_sampler.py`

---

### 8. **Complete File Reference for Adding New Parameters**

When adding a new sampling parameter, you would need to modify/update:

**Core Parameter Definition:**
1. `vllm/sampling_params.py` - Add field to SamplingParams class

**API Layer:**
2. `vllm/entrypoints/openai/protocol.py` - Add fields to ChatCompletionRequest and CompletionRequest
3. `vllm/config.py` - Add to available_params list if supporting HF generation config merge
4. `vllm/tracing.py` - Add tracing constant if needed

**V0 Metadata/Tensors:**
5. `vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` - Add to SamplingTensors class and from_sampling_metadata()
6. `vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` - Add to sampler logic and apply functions

**V1 Metadata/Tensors:**
7. `vllm/v1/sample/metadata.py` - Add to SamplingMetadata (V1)
8. `vllm/v1/worker/gpu_input_batch.py` - Add tensor arrays for parameter, add to _make_sampling_metadata()
9. `vllm/v1/sample/tpu/metadata.py` - Add to TPUSupportedSamplingMetadata if TPU-relevant
10. `vllm/v1/sample/sampler.py` - Update sampling logic

**Model Runners (12 files):** - Verify they work with updated metadata structure:
11. `vllm/worker/model_runner.py`
12. `vllm/worker/cpu_model_runner.py`
13. `vllm/worker/enc_dec_model_runner.py`
14. `vllm/worker/hpu_model_runner.py`
15. `vllm/worker/xpu_model_runner.py`
16. `vllm/worker/tpu_model_runner.py`
17. `vllm/worker/neuron_model_runner.py`
18. And other worker types (cpu_worker, etc.)

**Speculative Decode:**
19. `vllm/spec_decode/spec_decode_worker.py`
20. `vllm/spec_decode/medusa_worker.py`
21. `vllm/spec_decode/mlp_speculator_worker.py`

**Tests:**
22. `tests/test_sampling_params.py` - Add validation tests
23. `tests/samplers/test_sampler.py` - Add sampler integration tests
24. `tests/v1/sample/test_sampling_params_e2e.py` - Add V1 end-to-end tests

---

### 9. **Key Data Flow Summary**

```
SamplingParams (user input)
        ↓
ChatCompletionRequest.to_sampling_params()
        ↓
SequenceGroupMetadata (holds SamplingParams)
        ↓
V0 Path:
  SamplingMetadata.prepare()
    ↓
  SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata()
    (extracts top_p/top_k, creates tensors)
    ↓
  Sampler.forward()
    ↓
  _apply_top_k_top_p() or FlashInfer sampler

V1 Path:
  InputBatch._add_request()
    (stores in numpy arrays)
    ↓
  InputBatch._make_sampling_metadata()
    (creates V1 SamplingMetadata with tensors)
    ↓
  V1 Sampler.sample()
    ↓
  TopKTopPSampler kernel
```

This comprehensive mapping ensures that any new sampling parameter follows the same path through both V0 and V1 pipelines!

123	    """Sampling parameters for text generation.
124	
125	    Overall, we follow the sampling parameters from the OpenAI text completion
126	    API (https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/completions/create).
127	    In addition, we support beam search, which is not supported by OpenAI.
128	
129	    Args:
130	        n: Number of output sequences to return for the given prompt.
131	        best_of: Number of output sequences that are generated from the prompt.
132	            From these `best_of` sequences, the top `n` sequences are returned.
133	            `best_of` must be greater than or equal to `n`. By default,
134	            `best_of` is set to `n`. Warning, this is only supported in V0.
135	        presence_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on whether they
136	            appear in the generated text so far. Values > 0 encourage the model
137	            to use new tokens, while values < 0 encourage the model to repeat
138	            tokens.
139	        frequency_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on their
140	            frequency in the generated text so far. Values > 0 encourage the
141	            model to use new tokens, while values < 0 encourage the model to
142	            repeat tokens.
143	        repetition_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on whether
144	            they appear in the prompt and the generated text so far. Values > 1
145	            encourage the model to use new tokens, while values < 1 encourage
146	            the model to repeat tokens.
147	        temperature: Float that controls the randomness of the sampling. Lower
148	            values make the model more deterministic, while higher values make
149	            the model more random. Zero means greedy sampling.
150	        top_p: Float that controls the cumulative probability of the top tokens
151	            to consider. Must be in (0, 1]. Set to 1 to consider all tokens.
152	        top_k: Integer that controls the number of top tokens to consider. Set
153	            to 0 (or -1) to consider all tokens.
154	        min_p: Float that represents the minimum probability for a token to be
155	            considered, relative to the probability of the most likely token.
156	            Must be in [0, 1]. Set to 0 to disable this.
157	        seed: Random seed to use for the generation.
158	        stop: list of strings that stop the generation when they are generated.
159	            The returned output will not contain the stop strings.
160	        stop_token_ids: list of tokens that stop the generation when they are
161	            generated. The returned output will contain the stop tokens unless
162	            the stop tokens are special tokens.
163	        bad_words: list of words that are not allowed to be generated.
164	            More precisely, only the last token of a corresponding
165	            token sequence is not allowed when the next generated token
166	            can complete the sequence.
167	        include_stop_str_in_output: Whether to include the stop strings in
168	            output text. Defaults to False.
169	        ignore_eos: Whether to ignore the EOS token and continue generating
170	            tokens after the EOS token is generated.
171	        max_tokens: Maximum number of tokens to generate per output sequence.
172	        min_tokens: Minimum number of tokens to generate per output sequence
173	            before EOS or stop_token_ids can be generated
174	        logprobs: Number of log probabilities to return per output token.
175	            When set to None, no probability is returned. If set to a non-None
176	            value, the result includes the log probabilities of the specified
177	            number of most likely tokens, as well as the chosen tokens.
178	            Note that the implementation follows the OpenAI API: The API will
179	            always return the log probability of the sampled token, so there
180	            may be up to `logprobs+1` elements in the response.
181	        prompt_logprobs: Number of log probabilities to return per prompt token.
182	        detokenize: Whether to detokenize the output. Defaults to True.
183	        skip_special_tokens: Whether to skip special tokens in the output.
184	        spaces_between_special_tokens: Whether to add spaces between special
185	            tokens in the output.  Defaults to True.
186	        logits_processors: list of functions that modify logits based on
187	            previously generated tokens, and optionally prompt tokens as
188	            a first argument.
189	        truncate_prompt_tokens: If set to -1, will use the truncation size
190	            supported by the model. If set to an integer k, will use only
191	            the last k tokens from the prompt (i.e., left truncation).
192	            Defaults to None (i.e., no truncation).
193	        guided_decoding: If provided, the engine will construct a guided
194	            decoding logits processor from these parameters. Defaults to None.
195	        logit_bias: If provided, the engine will construct a logits processor
196	            that applies these logit biases. Defaults to None.
197	        allowed_token_ids: If provided, the engine will construct a logits
198	            processor which only retains scores for the given token ids.
199	            Defaults to None.
200	        extra_args: Arbitrary additional args, that can be used by custom
201	            sampling implementations, plugins, etc. Not used by any in-tree
202	            sampling implementations.
203	    """
204	
205	    n: int = 1
206	    best_of: Optional[int] = None
207	    _real_n: Optional[int] = None
208	    presence_penalty: float = 0.0
209	    frequency_penalty: float = 0.0
210	    repetition_penalty: float = 1.0
211	    temperature: float = 1.0

class SamplingParams(
        msgspec.Struct,
        omit_defaults=True,  # type: ignore[call-arg]
        # required for @cached_property.
        dict=True):  # type: ignore[call-arg]
    """Sampling parameters for text generation.

    Overall, we follow the sampling parameters from the OpenAI text completion
    API (https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/completions/create).
    In addition, we support beam search, which is not supported by OpenAI.

    Args:
        n: Number of output sequences to return for the given prompt.
        best_of: Number of output sequences that are generated from the prompt.
            From these `best_of` sequences, the top `n` sequences are returned.
            `best_of` must be greater than or equal to `n`. By default,
            `best_of` is set to `n`. Warning, this is only supported in V0.
        presence_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on whether they
            appear in the generated text so far. Values > 0 encourage the model
            to use new tokens, while values < 0 encourage the model to repeat
            tokens.
        frequency_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on their
            frequency in the generated text so far. Values > 0 encourage the
            model to use new tokens, while values < 0 encourage the model to
            repeat tokens.
        repetition_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on whether
            they appear in the prompt and the generated text so far. Values > 1
            encourage the model to use new tokens, while values < 1 encourage
            the model to repeat tokens.
        temperature: Float that controls the randomness of the sampling. Lower
            values make the model more deterministic, while higher values make
            the model more random. Zero means greedy sampling.
        top_p: Float that controls the cumulative probability of the top tokens
            to consider. Must be in (0, 1]. Set to 1 to consider all tokens.
        top_k: Integer that controls the number of top tokens to consider. Set
            to 0 (or -1) to consider all tokens.
        min_p: Float that represents the minimum probability for a token to be
            considered, relative to the probability of the most likely token.
            Must be in [0, 1]. Set to 0 to disable this.
        seed: Random seed to use for the generation.
        stop: list of strings that stop the generation when they are generated.
            The returned output will not contain the stop strings.
        stop_token_ids: list of tokens that stop the generation when they are
            generated. The returned output will contain the stop tokens unless
            the stop tokens are special tokens.
        bad_words: list of words that are not allowed to be generated.
            More precisely, only the last token of a corresponding
            token sequence is not allowed when the next generated token
            can complete the sequence.
        include_stop_str_in_output: Whether to include the stop strings in
            output text. Defaults to False.
        ignore_eos: Whether to ignore the EOS token and continue generating
            tokens after the EOS token is generated.
        max_tokens: Maximum number of tokens to generate per output sequence.
        min_tokens: Minimum number of tokens to generate per output sequence
            before EOS or stop_token_ids can be generated
        logprobs: Number of log probabilities to return per output token.
            When set to None, no probability is returned. If set to a non-None
            value, the result includes the log probabilities of the specified
            number of most likely tokens, as well as the chosen tokens.
            Note that the implementation follows the OpenAI API: The API will
            always return the log probability of the sampled token, so there
            may be up to `logprobs+1` elements in the response.
        prompt_logprobs: Number of log probabilities to return per prompt token.
        detokenize: Whether to detokenize the output. Defaults to True.
        skip_special_tokens: Whether to skip special tokens in the output.
        spaces_between_special_tokens: Whether to add spaces between special
            tokens in the output.  Defaults to True.
        logits_processors: list of functions that modify logits based on
            previously generated tokens, and optionally prompt tokens as
            a first argument.
        truncate_prompt_tokens: If set to -1, will use the truncation size
            supported by the model. If set to an integer k, will use only
            the last k tokens from the prompt (i.e., left truncation).
            Defaults to None (i.e., no truncation).
        guided_decoding: If provided, the engine will construct a guided
            decoding logits processor from these parameters. Defaults to None.
        logit_bias: If provided, the engine will construct a logits processor
            that applies these logit biases. Defaults to None.
        allowed_token_ids: If provided, the engine will construct a logits
            processor which only retains scores for the given token ids.
            Defaults to None.
        extra_args: Arbitrary additional args, that can be used by custom
            sampling implementations, plugins, etc. Not used by any in-tree
            sampling implementations.
    """

    n: int = 1
    best_of: Optional[int] = None
    _real_n: Optional[int] = None
    presence_penalty: float = 0.0
    frequency_penalty: float = 0.0
    repetition_penalty: float = 1.0
    temperature: float = 1.0
    top_p: float = 1.0
    top_k: int = 0
    min_p: float = 0.0
    seed: Optional[int] = None
    stop: Optional[Union[str, list[str]]] = None
    stop_token_ids: Optional[list[int]] = None
    ignore_eos: bool = False
    max_tokens: Optional[int] = 16
    min_tokens: int = 0
    logprobs: Optional[int] = None
    prompt_logprobs: Optional[int] = None
    # NOTE: This parameter is only exposed at the engine level for now.
    # It is not exposed in the OpenAI API server, as the OpenAI API does
    # not support returning only a list of token IDs.
    detokenize: bool = True
    skip_special_tokens: bool = True
    spaces_between_special_tokens: bool = True
    # Optional[list[LogitsProcessor]] type. We use Any here because
    # Optional[list[LogitsProcessor]] type is not supported by msgspec.
    logits_processors: Optional[Any] = None
    include_stop_str_in_output: bool = False
    truncate_prompt_tokens: Optional[Annotated[int, msgspec.Meta(ge=1)]] = None
    output_kind: RequestOutputKind = RequestOutputKind.CUMULATIVE

    # The below fields are not supposed to be used as an input.
    # They are set in post_init.

370	
371	
372	@dataclass
373	class SamplingTensors:
374	    """Tensors for sampling."""
375	
376	    temperatures: torch.Tensor
377	    top_ps: torch.Tensor
378	    top_ks: torch.Tensor
379	    min_ps: torch.Tensor
380	    presence_penalties: torch.Tensor
381	    frequency_penalties: torch.Tensor
382	    repetition_penalties: torch.Tensor
383	    prompt_tokens: torch.Tensor
384	    output_tokens: torch.Tensor
385	
386	    @classmethod
387	    def from_sampling_metadata(
388	        cls,
389	        sampling_metadata: "SamplingMetadata",
390	        vocab_size: int,
391	        device: torch.device,
392	        dtype: torch.dtype,
393	    ) -> tuple["SamplingTensors", bool, bool, bool]:
394	        prompt_tokens: list[array] = []
395	        output_tokens: list[array] = []
396	        top_ks: list[int] = []
397	        temperatures: list[float] = []
398	        top_ps: list[float] = []
399	        min_ps: list[float] = []
400	        presence_penalties: list[float] = []
401	        frequency_penalties: list[float] = []
402	        repetition_penalties: list[float] = []
403	        do_penalties = False
404	        do_top_p_top_k = False
405	        do_min_p = False
406	
407	        assert sampling_metadata.seq_groups is not None
408	        for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
409	            seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
410	            sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
411	            temperature = sampling_params.temperature
412	            p = sampling_params.presence_penalty
413	            f = sampling_params.frequency_penalty
414	            r = sampling_params.repetition_penalty
415	            top_p = sampling_params.top_p
416	            min_p = sampling_params.min_p
417	
418	            # k should not be greater than the vocab size.
419	            top_k = min(sampling_params.top_k, vocab_size)
420	            top_k = vocab_size if top_k < 1 else top_k
421	            if temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
422	                # NOTE: Zero temperature means deterministic sampling
423	                # (i.e., greedy sampling or beam search).
424	                # Set the temperature to 1 to avoid division by zero.
425	                temperature = 1.0
426	            if not do_top_p_top_k and (top_p < 1.0 - _SAMPLING_EPS
427	                                       or top_k != vocab_size):
428	                do_top_p_top_k = True
429	            if not do_min_p and min_p > _SAMPLING_EPS:
430	                do_min_p = True
431	            if not do_penalties and (abs(p) >= _SAMPLING_EPS
432	                                     or abs(f) >= _SAMPLING_EPS
433	                                     or abs(r - 1.0) >= _SAMPLING_EPS):
434	                do_penalties = True
435	
436	            is_prompt = seq_group.is_prompt
437	            if is_prompt and sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None:
438	                # For tokens in the prompt that we only need to get
439	                # their logprobs
440	                query_len = seq_group.query_len
441	                assert query_len is not None
442	                prefill_len = len(seq_group.prompt_logprob_indices)
443	                temperatures += [temperature] * prefill_len
444	                top_ps += [top_p] * prefill_len
445	                top_ks += [top_k] * prefill_len
446	                min_ps += [min_p] * prefill_len
447	                presence_penalties += [0] * prefill_len
448	                frequency_penalties += [0] * prefill_len
449	                repetition_penalties += [1] * prefill_len
450	
451	            if seq_group.do_sample:
452	                sample_lens = len(seq_group.sample_indices)
453	                assert sample_lens >= len(seq_ids)
454	                temperatures += [temperature] * sample_lens
455	                top_ps += [top_p] * sample_lens
456	                top_ks += [top_k] * sample_lens
457	                min_ps += [min_p] * sample_lens
458	                presence_penalties += [p] * sample_lens
459	                frequency_penalties += [f] * sample_lens
460	                repetition_penalties += [r] * sample_lens
461	
462	        if do_penalties:
463	            for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
464	                seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
465	                sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
466	                if (seq_group.is_prompt
467	                        and sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None):
468	                    prefill_len = len(seq_group.prompt_logprob_indices)
469	                    prompt_tokens.extend(
470	                        array(VLLM_TOKEN_ID_ARRAY_TYPE)
471	                        for _ in range(prefill_len))
472	                    output_tokens.extend(
473	                        array(VLLM_TOKEN_ID_ARRAY_TYPE)
474	                        for _ in range(prefill_len))
475	                if seq_group.do_sample:
476	                    for seq_id in seq_ids:
477	                        seq_data = seq_group.seq_data[seq_id]
478	                        prompt_tokens.append(seq_data.prompt_token_ids_array)
479	                        output_tokens.append(seq_data.output_token_ids_array)
480	
481	        sampling_tensors = SamplingTensors.from_lists(
482	            temperatures,
483	            top_ps,
484	            top_ks,
485	            min_ps,
486	            presence_penalties,
487	            frequency_penalties,
488	            repetition_penalties,
489	            prompt_tokens,
490	            output_tokens,
491	            vocab_size,
492	            device,
493	            dtype,
494	        )
495	        return (sampling_tensors, do_penalties, do_top_p_top_k, do_min_p)
496

@dataclass
class SamplingTensors:
    """Tensors for sampling."""

    temperatures: torch.Tensor
    top_ps: torch.Tensor
    top_ks: torch.Tensor
    min_ps: torch.Tensor
    presence_penalties: torch.Tensor
    frequency_penalties: torch.Tensor
    repetition_penalties: torch.Tensor
    prompt_tokens: torch.Tensor
    output_tokens: torch.Tensor

    @classmethod
    def from_sampling_metadata(
        cls,
        sampling_metadata: "SamplingMetadata",
        vocab_size: int,
        device: torch.device,
        dtype: torch.dtype,
    ) -> tuple["SamplingTensors", bool, bool, bool]:
        prompt_tokens: list[array] = []
        output_tokens: list[array] = []
        top_ks: list[int] = []
        temperatures: list[float] = []
        top_ps: list[float] = []
        min_ps: list[float] = []
        presence_penalties: list[float] = []
        frequency_penalties: list[float] = []
        repetition_penalties: list[float] = []
        do_penalties = False
        do_top_p_top_k = False
        do_min_p = False

        assert sampling_metadata.seq_groups is not None
        for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
            seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
            sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
            temperature = sampling_params.temperature
            p = sampling_params.presence_penalty
            f = sampling_params.frequency_penalty
            r = sampling_params.repetition_penalty
            top_p = sampling_params.top_p
            min_p = sampling_params.min_p

            # k should not be greater than the vocab size.
            top_k = min(sampling_params.top_k, vocab_size)
            top_k = vocab_size if top_k < 1 else top_k
            if temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
                # NOTE: Zero temperature means deterministic sampling
                # (i.e., greedy sampling or beam search).
                # Set the temperature to 1 to avoid division by zero.
                temperature = 1.0
            if not do_top_p_top_k and (top_p < 1.0 - _SAMPLING_EPS
                                       or top_k != vocab_size):
                do_top_p_top_k = True
            if not do_min_p and min_p > _SAMPLING_EPS:
                do_min_p = True
            if not do_penalties and (abs(p) >= _SAMPLING_EPS
                                     or abs(f) >= _SAMPLING_EPS
                                     or abs(r - 1.0) >= _SAMPLING_EPS):
                do_penalties = True

            is_prompt = seq_group.is_prompt
            if is_prompt and sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None:
                # For tokens in the prompt that we only need to get
                # their logprobs
                query_len = seq_group.query_len
                assert query_len is not None
                prefill_len = len(seq_group.prompt_logprob_indices)
                temperatures += [temperature] * prefill_len
                top_ps += [top_p] * prefill_len
                top_ks += [top_k] * prefill_len
                min_ps += [min_p] * prefill_len
                presence_penalties += [0] * prefill_len
                frequency_penalties += [0] * prefill_len
                repetition_penalties += [1] * prefill_len

            if seq_group.do_sample:
                sample_lens = len(seq_group.sample_indices)
                assert sample_lens >= len(seq_ids)
                temperatures += [temperature] * sample_lens
                top_ps += [top_p] * sample_lens
                top_ks += [top_k] * sample_lens
                min_ps += [min_p] * sample_lens
                presence_penalties += [p] * sample_lens
                frequency_penalties += [f] * sample_lens
                repetition_penalties += [r] * sample_lens

        if do_penalties:
            for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
                seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
                sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
                if (seq_group.is_prompt
                        and sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None):
                    prefill_len = len(seq_group.prompt_logprob_indices)
                    prompt_tokens.extend(
                        array(VLLM_TOKEN_ID_ARRAY_TYPE)
                        for _ in range(prefill_len))
                    output_tokens.extend(
                        array(VLLM_TOKEN_ID_ARRAY_TYPE)
                        for _ in range(prefill_len))
                if seq_group.do_sample:
                    for seq_id in seq_ids:
                        seq_data = seq_group.seq_data[seq_id]
                        prompt_tokens.append(seq_data.prompt_token_ids_array)
                        output_tokens.append(seq_data.output_token_ids_array)

        sampling_tensors = SamplingTensors.from_lists(
            temperatures,
            top_ps,
            top_ks,
            min_ps,
            presence_penalties,
            frequency_penalties,
            repetition_penalties,
            prompt_tokens,
            output_tokens,
            vocab_size,
            device,
            dtype,
        )
        return (sampling_tensors, do_penalties, do_top_p_top_k, do_min_p)

    @classmethod
    def from_lists(
        cls,
        temperatures: list[float],
        top_ps: list[float],
        top_ks: list[int],
        min_ps: list[float],
        presence_penalties: list[float],
        frequency_penalties: list[float],
        repetition_penalties: list[float],
        prompt_tokens: list[array],
        output_tokens: list[array],
        vocab_size: int,
        device: torch.device,
        dtype: torch.dtype,
    ) -> "SamplingTensors":
        # Note that the performance will be very bad without
        # pinned memory.
        pin_memory = is_pin_memory_available()

        do_penalties = prompt_tokens or output_tokens

        if do_penalties:
            prompt_t = make_tensor_with_pad(
                prompt_tokens,
                vocab_size,
                device="cpu",
                dtype=torch.int64,
                pin_memory=pin_memory,
            )
            output_t = make_tensor_with_pad(
                output_tokens,
                vocab_size,
                device="cpu",
                dtype=torch.int64,
                pin_memory=pin_memory,
            )
        else:
            empty_tensor = torch.empty(0, device=device, dtype=torch.long)
            prompt_t = empty_tensor
            output_t = empty_tensor

        temperatures_t = torch.tensor(
            temperatures,
            device="cpu",
            dtype=dtype,
            pin_memory=pin_memory,
        )
        top_ps_t = torch.tensor(
            top_ps,
            device="cpu",
            dtype=dtype,
            pin_memory=pin_memory,
        )
        min_ps_t = torch.tensor(
            min_ps,
            device="cpu",
            dtype=dtype,
            pin_memory=pin_memory,
        )
        presence_penalties_t = torch.tensor(
            presence_penalties,
            device="cpu",
            dtype=dtype,
            pin_memory=pin_memory,
        )
        frequency_penalties_t = torch.tensor(
            frequency_penalties,
            device="cpu",
            dtype=dtype,
            pin_memory=pin_memory,
        )
        repetition_penalties_t = torch.tensor(
            repetition_penalties,
            device="cpu",
            dtype=dtype,
            pin_memory=pin_memory,
        )
        top_ks_t = torch.tensor(
            top_ks,
            device="cpu",
            dtype=torch.int,
            pin_memory=pin_memory,
        )
        # Because the memory is pinned, we can do non-blocking
        # transfer to device.

        return cls(
            temperatures=temperatures_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
            top_ps=top_ps_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
            top_ks=top_ks_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
            min_ps=min_ps_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
            presence_penalties=presence_penalties_t.to(device=device,
                                                       non_blocking=True),
            frequency_penalties=frequency_penalties_t.to(device=device,
                                                         non_blocking=True),
            repetition_penalties=repetition_penalties_t.to(device=device,
                                                           non_blocking=True),
            prompt_tokens=prompt_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
            output_tokens=output_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
        )

self,
        logits: torch.Tensor,
        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
    ):
        """The goal here is to reuse sampling tensors between similar decode
        runs. This is possible because sampling logic does not change between
        decodes of the same sequences.
        """
        _, vocab_size = logits.shape

        # First free any existing stored sampling tensors.
        # This is necessary because some sampling tensors may
        # have pinned memory.
        self._sampling_tensors = None

        # Initialize new sampling tensors
        (sampling_tensors, do_penalties, do_top_p_top_k,
         do_min_p) = SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata(
             sampling_metadata, vocab_size, logits.device, logits.dtype)

        self._sampling_tensors = sampling_tensors
        self._do_penalties = do_penalties
        self._do_top_p_top_k = do_top_p_top_k
        self._do_min_p = do_min_p

    def forward(
        self,
        logits: torch.Tensor,
        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
    ) -> Optional[SamplerOutput]:
        """
        Single-step scheduling:
            * Perform GPU-side sampling computation & compute
            GPU-side logprobs tensor
            * Pythonize sampling result & logprobs tensor

        Multi-step scheduling:
            * Perform GPU-side sampling computation & compute
            GPU-side logprobs tensor
            * Defer Pythonization of sampling result & logprobs
            tensor
            * Encapsulate arguments required for deferred Pythonization
            in the
            [`SamplerOutput`][vllm.model_executor.layers.sampler.SamplerOutput]
            structure

        Args:
            logits: (num_tokens, vocab_size).
            sampling_metadata: Metadata for sampling.
        """
        assert logits is not None
        _, vocab_size = logits.shape

        # Prepare sampling tensors with pinned memory to avoid blocking.
        if not sampling_metadata.reuse_sampling_tensors:
            self._init_sampling_tensors(logits, sampling_metadata)
        elif self._do_penalties:
            # In this case, the sampling tensors logic depends on
            # "output_tokens" of a sequence. As a result, we cannot
            # reuse sampling tensors, since "output_tokens" changes
            # between decode runs.
            self._init_sampling_tensors(logits, sampling_metadata)

        assert self._sampling_tensors is not None
        sampling_tensors = self._sampling_tensors
        do_penalties = self._do_penalties
        do_top_p_top_k = self._do_top_p_top_k
        do_min_p = self._do_min_p

        logits = _apply_min_tokens_penalty(logits, sampling_metadata)

        # Apply presence and frequency penalties.
        if do_penalties:
            logits = apply_penalties(logits, sampling_tensors.prompt_tokens,
                                     sampling_tensors.output_tokens,
                                     sampling_tensors.presence_penalties,
                                     sampling_tensors.frequency_penalties,
                                     sampling_tensors.repetition_penalties)

        # Use float32 to apply temperature scaling.
        # Use in-place division to avoid creating a new tensor.
        logits = logits.to(torch.float)
        logits.div_(sampling_tensors.temperatures.unsqueeze(dim=1))

        if do_top_p_top_k and flashinfer_top_k_top_p_sampling is None:
            logits = _apply_top_k_top_p(logits, sampling_tensors.top_ps,
                                        sampling_tensors.top_ks)

        if do_min_p:
            logits = _apply_min_p(logits, sampling_tensors.min_ps)

        # We use float32 for probabilities and log probabilities.
        # Compute the probabilities.
        probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1, dtype=torch.float)
        # Compute the log probabilities.
        logprobs = torch.log_softmax(logits, dim=-1, dtype=torch.float)

        # Sample the next tokens.
        maybe_deferred_sample_results, maybe_sampled_tokens_tensor = _sample(
            probs,
            logprobs,
            sampling_metadata,
            sampling_tensors,
            include_gpu_probs_tensor=self.include_gpu_probs_tensor,
            modify_greedy_probs=self._should_modify_greedy_probs_inplace,
        )

        if self.include_gpu_probs_tensor:
            # Since we will defer sampler result Pythonization,
            # preserve GPU-side tensors in support of later
            # deferred pythonization of logprobs
            assert maybe_sampled_tokens_tensor is not None
            on_device_tensors = (probs, logprobs, maybe_sampled_tokens_tensor)
        else:
            # Since Pythonization has already happened, don't preserve
            # GPU-side tensors.
            on_device_tensors = None

        # Get the logprobs query results.
        prompt_logprobs = None
        sample_logprobs = None
        if not sampling_metadata.skip_sampler_cpu_output:
            # Pythonize logprobs now (GPU -> CPU); do not defer.
            assert not isinstance(maybe_deferred_sample_results,
                                  SampleResultArgsType)
            prompt_logprobs, sample_logprobs = get_logprobs(
                logprobs, sampling_metadata, maybe_deferred_sample_results)

        return _build_sampler_output(
            maybe_deferred_sample_results,
            sampling_metadata,
            prompt_logprobs,
            sample_logprobs,
            on_device_tensors=on_device_tensors,
            skip_sampler_cpu_output=sampling_metadata.skip_sampler_cpu_output)

    @property
    def _should_modify_greedy_probs_inplace(self) -> bool:
        """Whether or not the sampler should modify the probability distribution
        of greedily-sampled tokens such that multinomial sampling would sample
        the greedily-sampled token.

        In other words, if True then we set the probability of the greedily-
        sampled token to 1.

        This is used by speculative decoding, which requires that the sampling
        method be encoded into the probability distribution.
        """
        return self.should_modify_greedy_probs_inplace


def _apply_min_tokens_penalty(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """Apply min_tokens penalty which sets stop tokens to -inf if min_tokens
        have not been generated yet
    """
    # list of indices in logits that will be set to -inf
    logits_to_penalize: list[tuple[int, int]] = []

    # verifies that no rows in logits were missed unexpectedly

def _apply_top_k_top_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    p: torch.Tensor,
    k: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)

    # Apply top-k.
    top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
    # Get all the top_k values.
    top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
    top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))

    # Apply top-p.
    probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
    probs_sum = probs_sort.cumsum(dim=-1)
    top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
    # at least one
    top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))

    # Re-sort the probabilities.
    logits = torch.empty_like(logits_sort).scatter_(dim=-1,
                                                    index=logits_idx,
                                                    src=logits_sort)
    return logits


def _apply_min_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    min_p: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """
    Adapted from
    https://github.com/oobabooga/text-generation-webui/blob/3146124ec01f02c8fb1650a6517cf1b60b537aaf/modules/sampler_hijack.py#L16C17-L16C17
    """
    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
    scaled_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_min_p
    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))

    return logits


def _greedy_sample(
    selected_seq_groups: list[SequenceGroupToSample],
    samples: torch.Tensor,
) -> SampleResultType:
    """Run greedy sampling on a given samples.

    Args:
        selected_seq_groups: A list of sequence groups batched.
        samples: (num_selected_samples,) A tensor of samples. The length of
            samples could be smaller than selected_seq_groups if
            seq_group.do_sample is False.
    Returns:
        Tuple of (next_token_ids, parent_ids). The length of returned list is
        same as the length of selected_seq_groups. If the corresponding
        seq_group has do_sample=False, tuple contains ([], [])
    """
    samples_lst = samples.tolist()
    sample_idx = 0
    results: SampleResultType = []
    for seq_group in selected_seq_groups:
        if not seq_group.do_sample:
            results.append(([], []))
            continue

        seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
        num_parent_seqs = len(seq_ids)
